channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: LPG Shortage Prompts Chennai eateries to explore sustainable cooking options
item.description: A severe shortage of commercial LPG cylinders has hit restaurants, bakeries, and tea stalls in Chennai hard, forcing many to rethink their cooking methods. The crisis stems from disruptions in imports...

item.title: George Ruto Spends Time with Nairobi Biker Crew on Dirt Bike
item.description: George Ruto, the lastborn son of President William Ruto, has been spotted mingling with Nairobi's popular biker crew, 12 O' Clock Boyz. The young pilot, who is also skilled in flying helicopters, arri...

item.title: Moldova withstands Russian pressure and progresses towards EU membership
item.description: Since Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022, Moldova has faced significant challenges, yet it has managed to maintain stability and make notable progress. Despite Russian attempts t...

item.title: Trump seeks international support to protect shipping lanes amid Iran conflict
item.description: US President Donald Trump has called on other nations to assist in securing the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping route blocked by escalating tensions with Iran. The conflict, which has intensified f...

item.title: New Zealand Commemorates Victims of Christchurch Mosque Attacks Seven Years On
item.description: On 15th March 2026, New Zealand marked the seventh anniversary of the tragic attacks on the Al Noor Mosque and Linwood Islamic Centre in Christchurch, which occurred on 15th March 2019. During the att...

item.title: Philippine Women’s National Football Team eliminated by Japan in Asian Cup quarterfinals
item.description: The Philippine women's national football team, also known as the Filipinas, were eliminated in the quarterfinals of the 2026 AFC Women's Asian Cup after a hard-fought match against Japan. Despite show...

item.title: Letters criticise Australia's alignment with US-Israel military actions in the Middle East
item.description: A letter to the editor in The Canberra Times expresses deep concern over Australia's position in the current Middle East conflict, arguing that the nation is siding with actions that violate internati...

item.title: Lamu Youth Recounts Journey from Business Success to Financial Collapse and Recovery
item.description: Kabora Gratmay Magari, a young man from Lamu, once managed a flourishing online forex trading business worth nearly KSh 25 million when he was just 24 years old. Unfortunately, in May 2017, his busine...

item.title: Uzor Kalu assures unity in Abia APC as Deputy Speaker declares 2027 governorship ambition
item.description: Senator Orji Uzor Kalu wey dey represent Abia North don yan say no be true say APC for Abia dey divided. Na while e dey greet supporters for him house for Igbere, Bende Local Government Area e talk sa...

item.title: Trump warns of military strikes on Iran's oil facilities over Strait of Hormuz tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump don warn say if Iran no stop blocking ships for Strait of Hormuz, him fit strike the oil infrastructure for Kharg Island, the main hub wey handle almost 90% of Iran oil expor...

item.title: Pakistan Railways announces four Eid special trains to manage passenger rush
item.description: Pakistan Railways (PR) has announced the operation of four special trains ahead of the upcoming Eid festival to accommodate the large number of travellers wishing to visit their families. A spokespers...

item.title: Election Commission to Announce Poll Schedule in Press Conference Today
item.description: The Election Commission of India is scheduled to hold a press conference today at 4 PM to announce the dates for the upcoming elections. This announcement will detail the schedule for polling, includi...

item.title: Community Violence in Tongaat Following Alleged Sangoma Identification
item.description: In Egrondini, Tongaat, KwaZulu-Natal, a tragic incident unfolded after community members attacked three men who were allegedly identified by a sangoma as suspects in a recent burglary. The attack took...

item.title: Solarpunk: Building a Sustainable and Democratic Future
item.description: The article explores the solarpunk movement as a cultural and political framework for imagining and creating a sustainable, democratic, and technologically empowered future. It argues that humanity fa...

item.title: Judge allows Toronto Al-Quds rally to proceed after rejecting Ontario’s injunction request
item.description: A judge in Toronto has dismissed the Ontario government’s attempt to block the annual Al-Quds rally, allowing the demonstration to go forward as planned on Saturday. The province had sought an injunct...

item.title: Glovo launches rider safety feature and training to improve road safety in Nigeria
item.description: Glovo, the big tech company wey dey Nigeria, don join body with the Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) to launch new safety feature for their riders' app. Dem also start road safety training for Lagos, ...

item.title: ANC in Johannesburg decides to recall Mayor Dada Morero amid internal disputes
item.description: The African National Congress (ANC) in Johannesburg has resolved to recall Mayor Dada Morero following escalating tensions within the party. A special regional executive committee (REC) meeting on Sat...

item.title: Security forces eliminate six militants in Kohat amid rising violence in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
item.description: Six terrorists were killed in a joint Counter Terrorism Department (CTD) and police operation in the Lachi area of Kohat district, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, on Sunday. District Police Officer Shehbaz Elahi ...

item.title: Election Commission to release assembly election schedules for five states today
item.description: The Election Commission of India is scheduled to announce the assembly election schedule for five regions, namely West Bengal, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Assam, and Puducherry, today. A press conference has ...

item.title: Six Suspected Militants Neutralised in Kohat Operation by CTD and Police
item.description: In Kohat district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, a joint operation by the Counter-Terrorism Department (CTD) and local police successfully eliminated six terrorists, police officials reported. The operation t...
